title: 12 Days Southern Ethiopia Tours
html:
Southern Ethiopia Tours Tours

Mode of Transport: Surface

Duration: 12 Days / 11 Nights

Day 01: Arrival and Sightseeing in Addis Ababa

Arrival at Bole International Airport. Warm reception by your guide and transfer to the hotel for check-in. After a short break, enjoy a city tour of Addis Ababa. Overnight in Addis Ababa.

Day 02: Addis Ababa – Hawassa

Depart south from Addis Ababa via the Great Rift Valley. Stop along the way at Lake Ziway to admire the beautiful birdlife along the lake’s shores. Continue driving to Hawassa. Overnight stay in Hawassa.

Day 03: Hawassa – Dorze Village – Arba Minch

 In the morning, visit the lively Fish Market on Lake Hawassa. Then, continue to Arba Minch via Dorze village. The Dorze people live in the Guge Mountains near Arba Minch, in a cold and misty climate. They are renowned for their exceptional cotton weaving skills. Many traditional Ethiopian clothes originate from Chencha, the largest town in the area. Overnight stay in Arba Minch.

Day 04: Arba Minch – Lake Chamo – Jinka

In the morning, take a boat trip on Lake Chamo to visit the Crocodile Market and hippo pods. Also, visit Nech Sar National Park to see zebras and other wild animals. After the boat trip, drive to Jinka via Weyto (market day, Sunday) and Key Afar (market day: Thursday). Overnight in Jinka.

Day 05: Jinka – Mursi Village – Jinka

 Drive from Jinka to the Mursi village to visit the Mursi people, where women are famously known for inserting clay plates into their lips as a form of beauty. After the visit, drive back to Jinka and visit the South Omo Museum. Overnight stay in Jinka.

Day 06: Jinka – Dimeka – Turmi

Drive from Jinka to Turmi, home of the Hamer people. En route, stop at Dimeka (market days: Monday and Saturday) to visit the vibrant local markets. The Hamer and Dassanech tribes are renowned for their hairstyles, ornaments, scarification, and cultural heritage. At night, witness the traditional night dance of the Hamer people, known as Evangadi. Overnight stay in Turmi.

Day 07: Turmi – Omorate – Turmi

 In the morning, drive to Omorate and cross the Omo River by local boat to visit the Dassanech tribe. Afterward, return to Turmi. If a Jumping of the Bulls ceremony a traditional Hamer wedding rite is taking place, it will be visited. Monday is market day in Turmi. Overnight stay in Turmi.

Day 08: Day Trip to Karo Tribe

Take a day trip to visit the Karo tribe along the shores of the Omo River. The Karo are the smallest in number among the tribes of the Omo Valley and are especially known for their elaborate body decorations during local ceremonies. Overnight stay in Turmi.

Day 09: Turmi – Erbore – Konso

Depart from Turmi to Konso via Erbore in the Weyto Valley. If passing through on a Friday, visit the Erbore market. In Konso (market days: Mondays and Thursdays), explore the Konso people’s rich cultural heritage. They are known for their terraced agriculture and for sculpting wooden grave markers called “Waka.” The Konso also have a unique social organization, fascinating handicrafts, and distinctive architecture best experienced by trekking through the region. Overnight stay in Konso.

Day 10: Konso – Yabello

In the morning, visit a local Konso village and the “Natural New York Village,” so named for its tall sand formations that resemble skyscrapers. After the visit, continue driving to Yabello. Overnight stay in Yabello.

Day 11: Yabello – Dublock – El Sod – Lake Langano

After breakfast, continue south from Yabello to the Borena village of Dublock to visit the famous singing wells, where locals form chanting chains to haul water in buckets from deep wells. Then, proceed to El Sod, a crater lake where salt is traditionally mined. After the visit, drive to Lake Langano.
Overnight at Savana Lodge, Lake Langano.

Day 12: Lake Langano – Abiata-Shala National Park – Addis Ababa – Departure

After a refreshing morning swim in Lake Langano, depart to visit nearby Abiata-Shala National Park. Lake Shala is a deep crater lake (over 260 meters deep), while Lake Abiata is a soda lake that attracts thousands of flamingos. After the visit, continue driving back to Addis Ababa.

In the evening, enjoy a farewell dinner at one of Addis Ababa’s best traditional restaurants, where you can savor a variety of Ethiopian dishes and enjoy folkloric dance performances. After dinner, transfer to the airport for your international departure.
